sub_mechanisms: M_1: estimate the algebraic-degree-analog of the problem (proxy: an LLM-emitted integer in [1,K])
M_2: allocate CoT reasoning depth proportional to estimated degree
M_3: gate context window expansion to match required depth
M_4: verify with a solvability check (consistency)
stripped_llm_application: A CoT depth controller that uses an LLM-emitted algebraic-degree proxy as compute budget allocator; gates context-window expansion proportionally; runs a solvability-style consistency check at termination.
novelty_claim_stripped: Existing budget-forcing CoT (Wei 2022 etc.) uses fixed depth. Adaptive CoT uses confidence or value-network. The candidate uses an explicit algebraic-degree-proxy as an a-priori budget.
